title: 6.-Appointment of Public Health Officer – Dr. Noemi C. Doohan

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution to appoint Noemi C. Doohan, M.D., PhD, MPH, as the Public Health
Officer for the City of Berkeley.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
A full-time career benefited Health Officer position is budgeted for the current and
subsequent fiscal years.
sections.current_situation:
In accordance with state law, as a recognized local health jurisdiction, the City Council
must appoint a local Health Officer who has a medical degree and holds a license
issued by the Medical Board of California.
Dr. Doohan, a family physician, public health professional, and a Berkeley native, has
degrees from Mills College, UC Santa Barbara, Stanford, and the University of
Massachusetts Amherst. She completed her Family Medicine Residency at Contra
Costa County Regional Medical Center. Since 2019, Dr. Doohan has served as the
Public Health Officer for Lake and Mendocino Counties and as the Chief Medical Officer
for Santa Barbara County Public Health. She is also an elected Board Member of the
Executive Committee of the California Conference of Local Health Officers (CCLHO)
and is Co-Chair of CCLHO’s Health Equity Committee. Dr. Doohan brings a wealth of
experience to the City of Berkeley and the Health, Housing, and Community Services
Department is thrilled to have her join the team.
sections.background:
Since September 2023, the Health Officer position has been filled as an interim
appointment, while recruitment for the Permanent Health Officer was conducted. After
an interim period of assessing the current needs for this position and a rigorous search
with community input, Dr. Doohan was selected out of a very competitive field of
candidates. She will serve in partnership with Division Managers within the Health,

ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
State law requires City Council to appoint a physician Health Officer for our local health
jurisdiction.
sections.alternatives:
None. State law requires a physician Health Officer for our local health jurisdiction.
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Gilman, Director, Health, Housing, and Community Services, (510) 981-5400
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
APPOINTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH OFFICER – Dr. NOEMI C. DOOHAN
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley has performed local public health functions since 1890,
and is one of three City health jurisdictions in California; and
WHEREAS, local health jurisdictions must include a physician health officer; and
WHEREAS, mandatory functions of a local health jurisdiction include communicable
disease and tuberculosis control, public health emergency preparedness and response,
and registration of births and deaths; and
WHEREAS, these functions can only be performed under the authority of a physician
health officer; and
WHEREAS, Noemi C. Doohan, MD, is an experienced and exceptional public health
physician who meets the qualifications for this position.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that it
authorizes the appointment of Noemi C. Doohan, MD, as the Public Health Officer for the
City of Berkeley.
